steps.title: Identify both benefits
steps.content: The passage defines community science and presents two main benefits: it can promote community engagement through conservation efforts, and it can greatly increase the amount of data available to researchers.

steps.title: Reject one-sided summaries
steps.content: Choice A focuses on one study and invents surprise. Choice B contradicts the passage by saying community science is less likely to help researchers obtain accurate data. Choice D contradicts the passage by denying much effect on conservation efforts. The answer is C.
